title: Baha’i Citizen Shideh Tavakkoli Sentenced to Prison and Additional Punishments
content:
According to HRANA, Shideh Tavakkoli has been sentenced to imprisonment and additional punishments.

According to the ruling issued by Branch 15 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Abolghasem Salavati, Ms. Tavakkoli was sentenced to 10 years in prison, permanent deprivation of employment in government and public service, and a fine of 3.3 billion rials (330 million tomans) on charges of membership in anti-state groups and propaganda activities contrary to Islamic law. She was also sentenced to one additional year in prison on the charge of propaganda against the state.

The court further ordered the confiscation of two properties belonging to her in Tehran, valued at 18.7 billion tomans, as well as part of her personal belongings, including electronic devices, books, and religious items.

The verdict was issued by the court on May 20, 2026, and served on Ms. Tavakkoli on May 26, 2026.

Shideh Tavakkoli was arrested by security forces at her home in Tehran on May 27, 2025. After the completion of interrogations, she was temporarily released from prison on bail.
snippet: According to HRANA, Shideh Tavakkoli has been sentenced to imprisonment and additional punishments.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Arrest of Three Citizens by IRGC Intelligence Agents in Ravansar
content:
According to a report by HRANA, citing Kolbar News, three citizens have been arrested in Ravansar, Kermanshah province.

The identities of the detainees have been reported as Shahab Soleimani, a lawyer, and Khalil Parandak and Idris Golmohammadi.

According to the report, Shahab Soleimani, Khalil Parandak, and Idris Golmohammadi were arrested on Friday, July 17, by agents of the IRGC Intelligence Organization in Ravansar County.

As of the time of this report, no information has been obtained regarding the reasons for their arrests, the charges brought against them, or their place of detention.
snippet: According to a report by HRANA, citing Kolbar News, three citizens have been arrested in Ravansar, Kermanshah province.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Six Prisoners Executed in Prisons in Tabriz and Shushtar Prisons
content:
According to the Iran Human Rights Organization, a prisoner was executed at Shushtar Prison in the early hours of Wednesday, July 15. He has been identified as Amin Khodari Mirghaed, 28.

According to the report, Mr. Mirghaed had been arrested approximately four and a half years ago on murder charges and was subsequently sentenced to death by the judiciary.

The death sentences of five other prisoners were also carried out at Tabriz Prison. The prisoners have been identified as:

• Javad Abolfathi, 36, from Tabriz;
• Ezzat Khosrownejad, approximately 30, from Hashtrud;
• Sohrab Nourmohammadi, 40, from Jolfa;
• Sajjad Alizadeh, 31, from Varzaqan; and
• Fardin Barnal, 31, from Tabriz.

According to the report, Nourmohammadi, Alizadeh, and Bernal were executed in the early hours of Tuesday, July 14, at Tabriz Prison. They had been arrested in separate cases on drug-related charges and later sentenced to death by Revolutionary Courts.
snippet: According to the Iran Human Rights Organization, a prisoner was executed at Shushtar Prison in the early hours of Wednesday, July 15. He has been identified as Amin Khodari Mirghaed, 28.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Isfahan Shohada Square Case: 16 Citizens Stand Trial, Several Face “Enmity Against God” Charges
content:
According to HRANA, judicial proceedings are underway against 16 citizens arrested in connection with the January 2026 protests in a case known as the “Isfahan Shohada Square Case.”

Note: The “Isfahan Shohada Square Case” should not be confused with the separate “Isfahan Alikhani Square Case.” Although the two cases involve a similar number of defendants and comparable charges, they are distinct judicial proceedings. In the Alikhani Square case, several defendants were sentenced to death.

HRANA has verified the identities of 12 of the defendants. They are:

• Ahmadreza Saeedi
• Navid Elyasi
• Abolfazl Dadgostar
• Mehdi Mansouri
• Hamed Mehralian
• Mehdi Asadi
• Three brothers: Milad Booari, Mehrdad Booari, and Ali Booari
• Two 20-year-old twin sisters: Taraneh Rahimi and Romina Rahimi
• Setayesh Saedi

These citizens are currently being held in Dastgerd Prison and Dowlatabad Prison in Isfahan. The identities of the remaining four defendants had not been verified by HRANA at the time of publication.

According to information received by HRANA, the hearing for all 16 defendants was held on July 13, at Dastgerd Prison in Isfahan. The proceedings were video recorded.
snippet: According to HRANA, judicial proceedings are underway against 16 citizens arrested in connection with the January 2026 protests in a case known as the “Isfahan Shohada Square Case.”
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Concerns Mount Over Possible Executions as Alireza and Abolfazl Sepahi Remain in Solitary Confinement
content:
According to HRANA, Alireza Sepahi, aged approximately 25, and Abolfazl Sepahi, aged approximately 23, were taken from the general ward of Dastgerd Prison in Isfahan at 5:00 a.m. on Saturday, July 18, 2026, and transferred to solitary confinement.

The two prisoners are first cousins. One is married to the other’s sister, making them both cousins and brothers-in-law. They were arrested on January 10, 2026, in connection with the nationwide January protests.

According to information received by HRANA, the death sentences against Alireza and Abolfazl Sepahi were recently upheld by Iran’s highest judicial authority. However, their families remain unaware of the full details of the charges, the evidence in the case, and the judicial proceedings, while their lawyer has also been denied full access to the case file.

A source close to the families told HRANA:

“The lawyer said that after the death sentence was issued, an appeal was filed, and the verdict was later upheld by the Supreme Court. But even the lawyer was never given full access to the case file or a meaningful opportunity to pursue the case. Everywhere we go, no one provides any answers.”
snippet: According to HRANA, Alireza Sepahi, aged approximately 25, and Abolfazl Sepahi, aged approximately 23, were taken from the general ward of Dastgerd Prison in Isfahan at 5:00 a.m. on Saturday, July 18,
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Shiraz Revolutionary Court Sentences Fariba Hosseini to Prison and Additional Punishments
content:
According to HRANA, Fariba Hosseini has been sentenced to imprisonment.

According to a ruling issued on July 7, 2026, by Branch 1 of the Shiraz Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Sadati, and recently served on Ms. Hosseini, she was sentenced to six years in prison on the charge of “assembly and collusion to commit a crime against national security through supporting calls issued by groups opposed to the regime,” 30 months in prison for “insulting the Supreme Leader,” and 15 months in prison for “propaganda against the regime.” She was also sentenced to a two-year ban on leaving the country, including the revocation of her passport, as well as a two-year ban on online activities.

Notably, in this case, the court imposed sentences up to one-quarter above the maximum penalties prescribed by law for each charge. The ruling cites Articles 134 and 137 of the Islamic Penal Code, under which a judge may increase the statutory maximum sentence for each offense by up to one-quarter. The court also ruled that, among the sentences imposed, only the harshest sentence is enforceable.
snippet: According to HRANA, Fariba Hosseini has been sentenced to imprisonment.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Paria Dadgar Safa Sentenced to Prison by Appeals Court
content:
According to HRANA, Paria Dadgar Safahas been sentenced to imprisonment.

Based on information received by HRANA, Branch 36 of the Tehran Province Court of Appeals sentenced Ms. Dadgar Safa to 10 years in prison, with four years of the sentence suspended. She had previously been sentenced to 10 years in prison in the initial trial by Branch 28 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Mohammad-Reza Amouzad.

Ribwar Marabi, another defendant in the same case, was also handed a similar sentence. The cases of both defendants have been referred to the Evin Prosecutor’s Office Enforcement of Judgments Branch for implementation of the sentences.

The case against Ms. Dadgarsafa stems from the January 2026 protests.
snippet: According to HRANA, Paria Dadgar Safahas been sentenced to imprisonment.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Former Political Prisoner Ehsan Ghadiri Sentenced to One Year in Prison
content:
According to HRANA, Ehsan Ghadiri has been sentenced to imprisonment.

According to a ruling issued by Branch 7 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court on July 12, 2026, and formally served on Mr. Ghadiri yesterday, he was sentenced to one year in prison on the charge of propaganda against the regime.

The court hearing on Mr. Ghadiri’s case was held on July 11, 2026, before the same branch.

Mr. Ghadiri was violently arrested at a checkpoint in Tehran on April 2, 2026, and transferred to Greater Tehran Penitentiary. He was ultimately released on April 25, 2026, after posting bail.

Ehsan Ghadiri was previously released from Evin Prison in September 2024 after completing a one-year prison sentence.
snippet: According to HRANA, Ehsan Ghadiri has been sentenced to imprisonment.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: January 2026 Protests:Execution of Erfan Esfandiari and Gol-Mohammad Mohammadi
content:
According to HRANA, citing Mizan, the executions of Erfan Esfandiari and Gol-Mohammad Mohammadi, both detained during the January 2026 protests in Isfahan, were carried out at dawn on Sunday, July 19, 2026.

The Judiciary’s Media Center claimed that during the protests on January 8, 2026, in Alikhani Square in Isfahan, four law enforcement officers were killed. It further alleged that a number of protesters attacked police forces using firearms, bladed weapons, and incendiary materials, while also damaging public property and facilities. The judiciary additionally claimed that, after attacking the officers, the defendants committed acts of violence, including setting the victims’ bodies on fire, mutilating them, and filming the scenes.
snippet: According to HRANA, citing Mizan, the executions of Erfan Esfandiari and Gol-Mohammad Mohammadi, both detained during the January 2026 protests in Isfahan, were carried out at dawn on Sunday, July 19,
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Ten-Day Report on the New Wave of Attacks on Iran: Human Casualties and Disruption of Critical Infrastructure
content:
The collected data indicates that a significant portion of the attacks has been concentrated in southern Iran, particularly along the coasts of the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman. Military bases and defense facilities have been among the announced or reported targets. However, ports, piers, fishing vessels, bridges, railway lines, airports, telecommunications towers, and a number of infrastructures related to water, food, communications, transportation, and local economies have also been targeted or damaged as a result of the attacks.

Based on the cases that HRANA has documented and verified as of the time of publication, at least 48 people have been reported killed and 68 injured. In one of the most severe reported incidents, a one-year-old child in Bandar Abbas lost one hand after being struck by shrapnel, and the child’s condition has been reported as critical.
snippet: The collected data indicates that a significant portion of the attacks has been concentrated in southern Iran, particularly along the coasts of the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman. Military bases
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency
